raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need find all .php under /testbed, chmod 755, count. Use find -type f -name '*.php' -exec chmod 755 {} +; then count with wc -l. But need to print number changed. We can use find -print0 | tee /dev/null? Simpler: find ... -print | xargs chmod 755; then count lines. Use find ... -print | tee /dev/null | wc -l? Actually we need count after chmod. Use find ... -print | tee /dev/null | wc -l? But tee to /dev/null discards. Instead: find ... -print | tee /dev/null | wc -l? That prints count. But we also need to chmod. Use find ... -print0 | xargs -0 chmod 755; then count with find ... -type f -name '*.php' | wc -l? But that counts all php files, not changed. But we assume all changed. So command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 chmod 755; echo "Changed $(find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' | wc -l) files". Combine: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 chmod 755; echo "Changed $(find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' | wc -l) files". Provide single line? Use &&. Let's output single command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 chmod 755 && echo "Changed $(find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' | wc -l) files".
